info.title: Evervault API
info.description: The Evervault API allows developers to interact programmatically with their Evervault apps using HTTP requests.

tags.name: Insights
tags.description: The Evervault API provides several endpoints which can be used to retrieve additional information for cards and bin ranges. This can be used for tasks such as fraud detection and payment routing.

tags.name: 3D Secure
tags.description: 3D Secure is a security protocol designed to prevent fraud in online card transactions. It adds an additional layer of authentication during the payment process.

tags.name: Card Account Updates
tags.description: If your account has access to [Card Account Updater](/cards/card-account-updater) you can use the Card Account Updater API to register cards for automatic updates. If an update is available for a card, the `replacement` field will be populated with the identifier of the new card.

tags.name: Acquirers
tags.description: The Acquirer API allows you to enroll Acquirer details with Card Networks to enable use of other payment APIs such as [3D Secure](#3d-secure).

tags.name: Relays
tags.description: Relay is a network proxy that can be configured to encrypt or decrypt sensitive data as it passes between a client and a destination server. Relay can be used with your own API or with third-party APIs. It is designed to be easy to use and requires minimal changes to your existing code.

tags.name: Functions
tags.description: Evervault [Functions](/functions) are secure serverless functions which allow you to process data encrypted by Evervault products. When you pass encrypted data to a Function, it is automatically decrypted. You can then process this data by running custom logic written in Node.js or Python as you usually would, but without ever handling it in plaintext on your infrastructure.

tags.name: Client Tokens
tags.description: Client-Side Tokens are short-lived tokens that can be used to perform actions from your frontend applications.

post.description: The encrypt endpoint can be used to encrypt the values of a JSON object, or file. When encrypting the values of a JSON object the `Content-Type` header should be set to `application/json`, when encrypting files it should be set to `application/octet-stream`.

requestBody.description:
A JSON value or file to be encrypted. This can be any valid JSON
value: Objects, Arrays, Numbers, Boolean or Strings (strings should
be enclosed in double quotes).
requestBody.x-content:
A JSON value or file to be encrypted. This can be any valid JSON
value: Objects, Arrays, Numbers, Boolean or Strings (strings should
be enclosed in double quotes).

schema.description: The JSON value to be encrypted. This can be any valid JSON value: Dictionaries, Arrays, Numbers, Boolean or Strings (strings should be enclosed in double quotes).

200.x-content: An encrypted JSON object or file. For JSON requests, The payload structure will be the same as the one in the request payload, with all of the values encrypted.
200.description: An encrypted JSON object or file

post.description: The decrypt endpoint can be used to decrypt the values of a JSON object, or file. When decrypting the values of a JSON object the `Content-Type` header should be set to `application/json`, when decrypting files it should be set to `application/octet-stream`.

requestBody.description:
A JSON value or file to be decrypted. This can be any valid JSON
value: Objects, Arrays, Numbers, Boolean or Strings (strings should
be enclosed in double quotes).
requestBody.x-content:
A JSON value or file to be decrypted. This can be any valid JSON
value: Objects, Arrays, Numbers, Boolean or Strings (strings should
be enclosed in double quotes).

schema.description: The JSON value to be decrypted. This can be any valid JSON value: Dictionaries, Arrays or Strings (strings should be enclosed in double quotes). Non-encrypted values are returned unaltered.

200.x-content: A decrypted JSON object or file. For JSON requests, The payload structure will be the same as the one in the request payload, with any encrypted values decrypted.
200.description: A decrypted JSON value or file.

post.description:
Retrieve metadata for an encrypted value such as the time of encryption,
the type of data, the data role and category-specific metadata (e.g. card metadata)
without accessing the plaintext value.

token.description: The encrypted data to be inspected.

200.description: The metadata of the encrypted data that was submitted for inspection.

type.description: The type of the encrypted value.

category.description: The category or specific nature of the encrypted value.

encryptedAt.description: The date and time when the value was encrypted. This is a Unix timestamp in milliseconds. This field is currently only populated for values encrypted with a Data Role.

role.description: The data role of the encrypted value.

fingerprint.description: A unique identifier for the encrypted value.

metadata.description: Further metadata about the encrypted value. Returns different information based on the category of the data.

metadata.issuer: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

post.description: The Function run endpoint lets you invoke an [Evervault Function](/primitives/functions). The body of the request should contain a payload, the value of which will be decrypted and passed as an argument to the Function.

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: The name of the Function to be executed.

payload.description: The data payload that the Function will use during its execution. Any encrypted values will be decrypted before being passed to the function.

async.description: If you want your Function to run asynchronously and notify a callback URL, this can be set to `true` and the API will queue your Function run and return a `202` response code.

200.description: The Function run has completed

result.message: Hello from a Function! It seems you have 14 letters in your name

error.stack:
Error: Some error message!
    at exports.handler (/runtime/app/index.js:5:11)
    at /runtime/index.js:64:26
    at new Promise (<anonymous>)
    at /runtime/index.js:51:16

FailedRun.description: An error occurred during Function execution

202.description: The asynchronous Function invocation has been queued.

value.title: Request Timeout
value.detail: Function execution exceeded the allotted time and has timed out. Please review your code to ensure it finishes within the time limit set in function.toml.

409.description:
The Function is not ready to be invoked yet. This can occur when
it hasn't been executed in a while. Retrying to run the Function after a short
time should resolve this.

value.title: Function Not Ready
value.detail: The Function is not ready to be invoked yet. This can occur when it hasn't been executed recently. Please try again shortly.

post.description: Client-Side Tokens are restricted to specific payloads. By default, a Client-Side Token will expire after 5 minutes. The maximum expiration time of a token is 10 minutes. When using the REST API, the expiry field must be in epoch milliseconds.

action.description: The action that the token should permit

payload.description: The payload that the token must be used with

expiry.description: The expiry of the token in milliseconds format. Must be less than 10 minutes from now.

201.description: A client side token and its expiry

post.description: The Card Insights API allows you to perform address verification (AVS), name verification (ANI), and CVV verification checks. You can also check the card's push and pull transaction capabilities, as well as network and interchange fee estimates.

number.description: The card number. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted card number or a valid plaintext card number.

expiry.description: The card expiry. Required if the `address`, `cardholder`, or `cvv` extensions are requested.

cvv.description: The card security code. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted CVV or a valid plaintext CVV. Required if the `cvv` extension is requested.

extensions.description: The extensions to the card insight request. Example with all possible values - `["address", "cardholder", "cvv", "fees", "capabilities"]`

transaction.description: Details of the transaction. Required if the `fees` extension is requested.

amount.description: The amount of the payment in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).

currency.description: The currency of the payment, in lowercase ISO 4217 format.

cardholder.description: Details about the cardholder that the name verification (ANI) is for. Required if the `cardholder` extension is requested.

firstName.description: The first name of the cardholder

lastName.description: The last name of the cardholder

address.description: Details about the cardholder's address that the address verification (AVS) is for. Required if the `address` extension is requested.

state.description: State or province of the address. Required when `country` is `us`, `ca`, or `au`. Must use the ISO 3166-2 code (subdivision only). For example, for New York (`us-ny`), provide `ny`.

country.description: The ISO 3166-1 alphabetic 2 character country code.

bin.issuer: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

post.description: Performs a BIN lookup for the provided card number and retrieves associated information such as the card brand, country, issuer, and more.

requestBody.description: The BIN (Bank Identification Number) lookup request body, which includes the card number.

number.description:
The card number for which the BIN lookup is being requested.
It can be a plaintext Card number (FPAN) / Network Token number (DPAN), an encrypted
Card number / Network Token number or simply just a BIN (6-10 first digits of a Card Number) for range lookup.

201.description: Returns BIN details for the provided card number.
201.x-content: Returns the [BIN Lookup object](#the-bin-lookup-object) that was created.

value.issuer: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

post.description:
Enrolls a Merchant with Card Networks.

In rare cases it can take up to 48 hours to enroll a merchant with each of the card networks. If you are still unable to provision network tokens after this period, please contact support@evervault.com

name.description: The official name of the Merchant as recognized in transactions and communications. This name is used for display purposes and may be the company's trade name or a derived nickname.

website.description: The official website URL of the Merchant.

business.description: The business details of the Merchant.

legalName.description: The legal name under which the Merchant's business is registered.

address.description: The physical address of the Merchant's principal place of business.

networkTokens.description: The Merchant's Network Tokens configuration. This field should only be populated if the Merchant has already been enrolled in one of the Card Network programs and can use an existing Token Requestor ID (TRID).

cardBrand.description: The Card Brand for which the configuration belongs to.

tokenRequestorIdentifier.description: The existing Token Requestor ID assigned by the Card Brand.

domains.description: The domains the Merchant wants registered with Apple Pay. Only the domain name is required, without the protocol or path. For example, `example.com` is a valid domain, but `https://example.com` is not.

get.description: Lists all Merchants that have been created.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve.

200.description: Returns a list of Merchant objects.

get.description: Retrieves a Merchant by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the merchant.

200.description: Returns a Merchant object.

patch.description: Updates a Merchant by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: The id of the Merchant to be updated.

domains.description: The domains the Merchant wants registered with Apple Pay. To remove a domain previously registered with Apple Pay, exclude it from the list. Only the domain name is required, without the protocol or path. For example, `example.com` is a valid domain, but `https://example.com` is not.

200.description: Returns the updated Merchant object.

delete.description: Deletes a Merchant by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: merchant_id
parameters.description: The id of the Merchant to be deleted.

post.description: Creates an Acquirer to be used with 3DS sessions.

default.description: Specifies whether this Acquirer is the default. Only one default Acquirer configuration can exist at a time; setting a new one clears the previous default. If none is defined, the first Acquirer created becomes the default. See the [Acquirers API](/api#acquirers) for details.

network.description: The card network of the Acquirer.

bin.description: The Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the Acquirer.

acquirerMerchantIdentifier.description: The merchant identifier associated with the configuration.

country.description: The country of the Acquirer configuration (ISO 3166 Alpha-2 code). This is the country that will be used when creating 3DS sessions.

get.description: Lists all Acquirers that have been created.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve.

200.description: Returns a list of Acquirer objects.

get.description: Retrieves an Acquirer by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: acquirer_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Acquirer.

200.description: Returns an Acquirer object.

patch.description: Updates an Acquirer by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: acquirer_id
parameters.description: The id of the Acquirer to be updated.

default.description: Specifies whether this Acquirer is the default. Only one default Acquirer configuration can exist at a time; setting a new one clears the previous default. See the [Acquirers API](/api#acquirers) for details.

configurations.description: The configurations of the Acquirer. The configuration is identified by its network.

network.description: The card network to use the configuration for.

bin.description: The Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the acquirer.

acquirerMerchantIdentifier.description: The merchant identifier the configuration is associated with.

country.description: The country of the acquirer configuration (upper or lowercase ISO 3166 Alpha-2 code). This is the country that will be used when creating 3DS sessions.  Please note that the country is stored in lowercase.

200.description: Returns the updated Acquirer object.

delete.description: Deletes an Acquirer by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: acquirer_id
parameters.description: The id of the Acquirer to be deleted.

post.description: Create a Network Token for a given card.

number.description: The card number. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted card number or a valid plaintext card number.

cvc.description: The card security code. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted CVV or a valid plaintext CVV.

merchant.description: The unique identifier of the Merchant previously created using the Evervault API. It denotes the Merchant to which the Network Token should be associated with.

201.x-content: Returns the [Network Token object](#the-network-token-object) that was created.

EvervaultEncryptedExample.summary: Network Token created with encrypted card details

card.issuer: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

PlaintextCardExample.summary: Network Token created with plaintext card details

400.description: The request was invalid or cannot be otherwise served.

value.title: Ineligible Card
value.detail: The card is not eligible for tokenization. This may be due to the card issuer not supporting tokenization.

value.title: Invalid Card
value.detail: The provided card details did not pass verification checks. This may be due to an invalid card number, an incorrect CVC, or a discrepancy between the provided and actual card expiry date.

value.title: Declined Tokenization
value.detail: Tokenization for the given card was declined by the card issuer. This may be due to restrictions related to the card type, issuer policies, or the card's current status

value.title: Token Service Provider Provisioning
value.detail: The Token Service Provider is currently provisioning. This may take up to 48 hours to complete. Please try again later. Contact support@evervault.com if this continues after 48 hours.

500.description: An error occurred while processing the request.

value.title: Unavailable Token Service Provider
value.detail: The Token Service Provider is unavailable. Please try again later.

post.description: Creates a 3DS Session to initiate the 3DS Authentication process. The session ID must then be passed to our client-side SDK to complete the authentication process. [Learn more](/sdks/javascript#ui.threedsecure())

categoryCode.description: The category code of the merchant.

country.description: The country code of the merchant.

number.description: The card number. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted card number or a valid plaintext card number.

acquirer.description: The identifier of the acquirer configuration. See the [Acquirers API](/api#acquirers) for details.

customer.description: The details of the customer who initiated the transaction.

phone.description: The phone number of the customer.

countryCode.description: The numerical country calling code of the phone number.

number.description: The phone number of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

shipping.description: The shipping details of the customer.

billing.description: The billing details of the customer.

identifier.description: The tax identifier of the cardholder.

payment.description: The payment details of the 3D Secure Authentication. This field is mandatory for transactions involving payment authentications but not required for non-payment authentications.

preferredVersions.description:
A prioritized list of preferred 3DS versions. The first version in the list is the most preferred. If the first version is not supported, the next version in the list is attempted. If no preferred version is provided, the most optimal version will be automatically selected. If none of the specified versions are supported by the issuer, the session will fail.

Supported versions: 2.2.0

201.x-content: Returns the [3DS Session object](#the-3ds-session-object) that was created.

transStatus.detail: Challenge Required; Additional authentication is required using the CReq/CRes
Not Authenticated/Account Not Verified; Transaction denied

parameters.name: 3ds_session_id
parameters.description: The id of the session

200.description: Returns a 3DS Session object.

transStatus.detail: Challenge Required; Additional authentication is required using the CReq/CRes

get.description: Retrieve messages for a 3DS Session. This endpoint can be helpful for debugging 3DS sessions, providing insight into the raw 3DS messages that were exchanged between the 3DS Server and the Directory Server.

parameters.name: 3ds_session_id
parameters.description: The id of the 3DS session

200.description: Returns 3DS messages for the session.
200.x-content: Returns an array of [3DS Message objects](#the-3ds-message-object).

messages.description: Array of 3DS messages for the session

type.description: The type of 3DS message (e.g., AReq, ARes, CReq, CRes, RReq, RRes)

created_at.description: Timestamp when the message was created

post.description:
When registering a card, you can provide either an Evervault encrypted card number or a plaintext card number. If you provide a plaintext card number it will be encrypted and the encrypted value will be returned in the response.

Card registration is idempotent on card number and expiry date. Multiple requests to register a card containing the same request body will return the same Card object.

number.description: The card number. This should be a valid Evervault encrypted card number or a valid plaintext card number.

200.description: Returns an existing Card object.
200.x-content: Returns the [Card object](#the-card-object) that was created.

Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

post.description: Create a Webhook Endpoint

url.description: The URL of the Webhook Endpoint.

events.description:
A list of Events that the Webhook Endpoint is subscribed to. The following events are supported:
  - `payments.merchant.updated`: A Merchant was updated
  - `payments.network-token.updated`: A Network Token was updated
  - `function.run.completed`: An asynchronous Function Run was completed

parameters.description: The identifier of the last Webhook Endpoint in the previous page of results.

200.x-content: Returns a list of [Webhook Endpoint objects](#the-webhook-endpoint-object).

hasMore.description: Indicates whether there are more Webhook Endpoints to retrieve.

parameters.name: webhook_endpoint_id

200.description: The Webhook Endpoint was retrieved successfully.

  - `*`: All Events

  - `payments.network-token.updated`: A Network Token was updated

ApiKey.description: Authentication using an API key. The username is the App ID and the password is the Api Key.

TokenAuth.description: Authentication using a short lived run token that you can share with clients. The Authorization header must be formatted as follow: "RunToken <Function Run Token>"

ClientSideToken.description: Authentication using a short lived token that you can share with clients. The Authorization header must be formatted as follow: "Token <Client-Side Token>"

CardBrand.description: The card brand associated with the payment card.

CardFunding.description:
The card funding type specifies the method by which transactions are
financed. `debit` refers to cards that draw funds directly from a linked bank account,
typically used for immediate payment. `credit` indicates cards that provide a line
of credit from which users can borrow funds for transactions, to be repaid later
under the terms of the credit agreement. `prepaid` are cards loaded with a set amount
of funds in advance and can be used until the balance is depleted. `deferred-debit`
cards combine aspects of debit and credit cards, allowing transactions to be debited
from a linked account at a later date, usually monthly. `charge` cards require full
payment of the balance at the end of each billing cycle, but do not have a pre-set
spending limit.

CardSegment.description:
The card segment indicates the primary market or usage category of the
card. Each segment caters to specific needs and functionalities: `consumer` for
personal use cards, offering rewards and benefits for everyday purchases; `commercial`
for cards used by large organizations or corporations with features like higher
credit limits and expense tracking; `business` for small to medium-sized business
use, providing simpler accounting integration and business spending rewards; `government`
for cards used by government entities, complying with governmental financial regulations
and controls; `payouts` for cards designed to disburse payments like payroll or
cashback; and `all` for general-purpose cards not confined to a specific segment.

CardMetadata.description: Card Metadata (Only present if category is `card-number`).

bin.description: The first 6 or 8 digits of the card number.

lastFour.description: The last 4 digits of the card number.

country.description: The country where the card was issued in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

currency.description: The currency of the card in ISO 4217 format.

issuer.example: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific Function execution instance.

status.description: The outcome of the Function execution. A 'success' denotes successful execution, whereas a 'failure' indicates that the Function encountered an error.

result.description: This field represents the output returned by the Function. This is provided only when the Function execution status is 'success'.

error.description: This field details any error that occurred during Function execution. This is present only if the status is 'failure'.

message.description: A concise explanation of the error that occurred during Function execution.

stack.description: A trace detailing the sequence of events leading to the error, useful for debugging purposes.

Error: Some error message!

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Function execution was triggered.

code.description: A distinct error code, presented in slug format, that identifies a specific error.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the error.

status.description: The HTTP status code for the error.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation of the error.

pointer.description: The JSON pointer to the field that caused the error.

CardFastFunds.description:
Specifies the availability of instant payment capabilities for card transactions,
indicating whether funds can be transferred immediately both within the same country
and across international borders.

domestic.description:
When true, indicates that instant payments are supported for transactions
within the same country/domestic market. This allows for immediate funds
availability for local transfers.

crossBorder.description:
When true, indicates that instant payments are supported for international
transactions between different countries. This enables immediate funds
availability for cross-border transfers.

CardThreeDSMetadata.description:
Metadata about 3DS capabilities, including supported versions 
and ACS (Access Control Server) indicators.

supportedVersions.description: The 3DS versions supported by the ACS and DS

items.description: Array of 3DS versions supported by the Access Control Server
Array of 3DS versions supported by the Directory Server

code.description:
The indicator code, the following values are supported:

- `authentication-available`
- `attempts-supported`
- `decoupled-authentication-supported`
- `trustlist-supported`
- `device-binding-supported`
- `webauthn-authentication-supported`
- `spc-authentication-supported`
- `tra-exemption-supported`
- `trustlist-exemption-supported`
- `low-value-exemption-supported`
- `secure-corporate-payments-exemption-supported`
- `emvco-reserved`
- `ds-reserved`

**American Express only:**
- `issuer-tra-exemption-supported`
- `issuer-trustlist-exemption-supported`
- `issuer-low-value-exemption-supported`
- `issuer-secure-corporate-payments-exemption-supported`
- `bridging-message-extension-supported`

**Mastercard only:**
- `smart-auth-direct-stand-in-only`
- `smart-auth-direct`
- `payment-transactions-supported`
- `non-payment-transactions-supported`
- `app-channel-supported`
- `browser-channel-supported`
- `app-acs-challenge-supported`
- `browser-acs-challenge-supported`

**Visa only:**
- `issuer-tra-exemption-supported`
- `data-only-supported`
- `delegated-authentication-supported`
- `digital-auth-framework-supported`

indicator.description: The raw numeric indicator value, ranges from 01 to 99

line1.description: The first line of the address.

line2.description: The second line of the address.

city.description: The city where the address is located.

state.description: State or province of the address. Required when `country` is `us`, `ca`, or `au`. Must use the ISO 3166-2 code (subdivision only). For example, for New York (`us-ny`), provide `ny`.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

country.description: The country where the address is located, in lowercase ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

enrolment.description: An array detailing the Merchant's enrollment status for network token issuance across different card brands.

cardBrand.description: The card brand (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express) for which the enrollment status is being described. Currently, Network Tokens is only supported for Visa, Mastercard and American Express.

tokenRequestorIdentifier.description: The unique Token Requestor Identifier (TRID) associated with the Merchant for the specific card brand. This identifier is used when creating Network Tokens for the Merchant.

status.description: The current stage of the Merchant's enrollment process for network token issuance with the specified card brand. `active` means the Merchant is fully enrolled and can create Network Tokens for the card brand.

domains.description: The domains the Merchant has enrolled with Apple Pay and the status of the enrollment.

domain.description: The top-level domain name of the Merchant's website.

status.description:
The current status of the Merchant's Apple Pay enrollment for the
specified domain. `active` means the Merchant is fully enrolled and
can accept Apple Pay payments on the domain.

id.description: A unique identifier assigned to each Merchant.

name.description: The official name of the Merchant as recognized in transactions and communications. This name is used for display purposes and may be the company's trade name or a derived nickname.

website.description: The official website URL of the Merchant.

business.description: The business details of the Merchant.

legalName.description: The legal name under which the Merchant's business is registered.

address.description: The physical address of the Merchant's principal place of business.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Merchant was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Merchant was last updated.

id.description: The unique identifier of the acquirer configuration.

name.description: The name of the acquirer configuration.

description.description: The description of the acquirer configuration.

network.description: The card network of the Acquirer.

bin.description: The Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the Acquirer.

acquirerMerchantIdentifier.description: The merchant identifier the configuration is associated with.

country.description: The country of the acquirer configuration (upper or lowercase ISO 3166 Alpha-2 code). This is the country that will be used when creating 3DS sessions.  Please note that the country is stored in lowercase.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Acquirer was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Acquirer was last updated.

month.description: The card expiry month, in MM format (e.g. 12 for December)

year.description: The card expiry year, in YY format (e.g. 26 for 2026)

year.description: The year of the Network Token's expiry date, in YY format (e.g. 26 for 2026)

card.description: The details of the underlying encrypted card.

lastFour.description: The last four digits of the card number.

expiry.description: The expiry details of the card.

month.description: The month of the card's expiry date, in MM format (e.g. 12 for December)

year.description: The year of the card's expiry date, in YY format (e.g. 26 for 2026)

brand.description: The card brand associated with the payment card.

tokenRequestorIdentifier.description: The identifier of the Token Requestor (TRID) that requested the Network Token.

tokenServiceProvider.description: The Token Service Provider (TSP) that issued the Network Token.

paymentAccountReference.description: The unique identifier of the Payment Account associated with this Network Token.

status.description: The status of the Network Token. Active means the Network Token is valid and can be used for payments.

merchant.description: The unique identifier of the Merchant associated with this Network Token.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Network Token was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Network Token was last updated.

id.description: A unique identifier representing a specific Network Token Cryptogram.

cryptogram.description: The value of the Network Token Cryptogram. This is the value that is used embedded in the Authorization request.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Network Token Cryptogram was created.

type.description: The MIME type of the card art image.

data.description: The base64-encoded image data of the card art.

width.description: The width of the card art image in pixels.

height.description: The height of the card art image in pixels.

PaymentOneOff.description: A payment that is made in a single transaction.

amount.description: The amount of the payment in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents).

currency.description: The currency of the payment, in lowercase ISO 4217 format.

PaymentInstallment.description: A repeating payment for paying a total amount in multiple installments.

amount.description: The amount of the payment in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents).

currency.description: The currency of the payment, in lowercase ISO 4217 format.

frequency.description: Indicates the minimum time between authorizations.

expiry.description: The epoch time in seconds when the installment expires. Maximum value is 4102394400 (equivalent to 2099-12-31).

PaymentRecurring.description: A repeating payment with a regular interval.

amount.description: The amount of the payment in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents).

currency.description: The currency of the payment, in lowercase ISO 4217 format.

frequency.description: Indicates the minimum time between authorizations.

expiry.description: The epoch time in seconds when the recurring payment expires. Maximum value is 4102394400 (equivalent to 2099-12-31).

reason.description: The reason for not requesting a challenge. This value should only be present when the preference is `no-challenge-requested`.

ThreeDSInitiator.description: Details about the transaction initiation process.

reason.description: The reason for the transaction to be initiated by the merchant. This field is mandatory when the `type` is `merchant`.

initialSession.description:
The ID of the initial session. This can either be an Evervault 3DS Session ID or a `threeDSServerTransactionID`. This field should be provided if the merchant has previously authenticated the cardholder using 3DS.
The API does not currently enforce this requirement, but it is recommended to always provide this field. In some cases Authentications will error if this field is not populated.

bin.description: The Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the acquirer.

merchantIdentifier.description: The merchant identifier assigned by the acquirer.

country.description: The country code of the acquirer (upper or lowercase ISO 3166 Alpha-2 code).  Please note that the country is stored in lowercase.

ThreeDSAccessControlServer.description: Details about the Access Control Server involved in the 3DS transaction.

transactionIdentifier.description: The Access Control Server's transaction identifier.

referenceNumber.description: The Access Control Server's reference number assigned by EMVCo.

ThreeDSDirectoryServer.description: Details about the Directory Server involved in the 3DS transaction.

referenceNumber.description: The Directory Server's reference number assigned by EMVCo.

network.description: The network of the Directory Server.

ThreeDSSession.description: The 3D Secure API can be used in combination with our [Client-Side SDK's](/sdks/javascript#uithreedsecure) to authenticate cardholders using 3D Secure. This API allows you to create, retrieve and update 3D Secure sessions.

id.description: A unique identifier assigned to each 3DS Authentication.

lastFour.description: The last four digits of the card number.

issuer.description: The name of the card issuer.

country.description: The country code of the card. (ISO 3166 Alpha-2 code)

currency.description: The currency code of the card. (ISO 4217 three-letter code)

categoryCode.description: The category code of the merchant.

country.description: The country code of the merchant.

payment.description: The payment details of the 3D Secure Authentication.

threeDSServer.description: Details about the 3DS Server involved in the 3DS transaction.

transactionIdentifier.description: The unique identifier for the 3DS transaction.

version.description: The 3D Secure version used to authenticate the session.

authentication.description: The details of the 3DS Authentication. This field is present when the status is `success`.

methods.description: The authentication method(s). This field is only present when the flow is `challenge`.

nextAction.description: The next action required to complete the 3DS Authentication.

type.description: The next action required to progress the 3DS Authentication. If the type is `use-sdk` you must use our [Client-Side SDK](/sdks/javascript#ui.threedsecure()) to complete the 3DS Authentication.

cryptogram.description: The 3DS cryptogram (also called Authentication Value). This value must be retrieved and provided to the payment gateway when processing the payment. This value is only present when the status is `success` and is retained for one hour.

eci.description: The details of the Electronic Commerce Indicator. This value is only present when the status is `success`.

value.description:
The value of the ECI. Below are descriptions of each of the possible values:

Mastercard:
  - `00`: Not authenticated. No liability shift.
  - `01`: Attempted (Stand-in) authentication. Liability shift applies.
  - `02`: Successful authentication. Liability shift applies.
  - `04`: Data only authentication. No liability shift.
  - `06`: Transaction is exempt from SCA. No liability shift.
  - `07`: Recurring transaction authenticated. Liability shift applies for initial transaction only.

Visa, American Express, Discover, Diners Club, JCB:
  - `05`: Successful authentication. Liability shift applies.
  - `06`: Attempted (Stand-in) authentication. Liability shift applies.
  - `07`: Not authenticated. No liability shift.

descriptor.description: The descriptor of the ECI. This value must be retrieved and provided to the payment gateway when processing the payment.

liabilityShift.description: A boolean value indicating whether the liability shift has occurred.

failureReason.description:
The reason for the 3DS Authentication failure. This field is present when the status is `failure` and can be used for troubleshooting.
Below are descriptions of each of the possible values:

- `failed-authentication`: The authentication failed. Typically this is due to the cardholder failing to provide the correct 3DS authentication details. This transaction should be considered as potentially fraudulent.
- `card-not-enrolled`: The card is not enrolled in 3DS and therefore cannot be authenticated.
- `suspected-fraud`: The transaction is suspected to be fraudulent. You should not process this transaction.
- `invalid-acquirer-details`: The acquirer details are invalid. Please check the `acquirer` object for the correct details.
- `transaction-cancelled-by-cardholder`: The cardholder explicitly cancelled the transaction.
- `unsupported-3ds-version`: The 3DS version is not supported.
- `challenge-required`: This failure mode indicates that the cardholder is required to complete a challenge but the SDK has been mounted with the `failOnChallenge` option set to `true`.
- `authentication-timed-out`: The authentication timed out. This is likely because the cardholder abandoned the transaction.
- `acs-unavailable`: The Access Control Server associated with the card issuer was unavailable. Please try again later.
- `directory-server-unavailable`: The Directory Server associated with the card network was unavailable. Please try again later.
- `transient-system-failure`: A transient system failure occurred. This is likely due to a technical issue with the 3DS authentication process. Please try again later.
- `invalid-card-details`: The card details are invalid. Please check the `card` object for the correct details and ensure the card is not expired.
- `transaction-not-permitted`: The current session configuration is not permitted for this card. For example, defining a merchant intiated transaction reason of `secure-corporate-payment` for non-commercial cards.
- `protocol-error`: A protocol error occurred. This is likely due to a technical issue with the 3DS authentication process. Please try again later.

ares.description: The details of the 3DS Authentication Response (ARes).

value.description: The raw value of the transaction status.

detail.description: The detailed description of the transaction status.

transStatusReason.description: The reason for the transaction status if transStatus is 'N', 'U' or 'R'.

value.description: The raw value of the transaction status reason.

detail.description: The detailed description of the transaction status reason.

cres.description: The details of the 3DS Challenge Response (CRes).

value.description: The raw value of the transaction status.

detail.description: The detailed description of the transaction status.

rreq.description: The result of the 3DS authentication when a challenge has occurred.

value.description: The raw value of the transaction status.

detail.description:
The detailed description of the transaction status. Below are descriptions of each of the possible values:
- `Y` - Authentication Verification Successful.
- `N` - Not Authenticated/Account Not Verified; Transaction denied.
- `U` - Authentication/Account Verification Could Not Be Performed; Technical or other problem.
- `A` - Attempts Processing Performed; Not Authenticated/Verified, but a proof of attempted authentication/verification is provided.
- `R` - Authentication/Account Verification Rejected; Issuer is rejecting.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this 3DS-Session was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this 3DS-Session was last updated.

id.description: The unique identifier for the card.

number.description: The Evervault encrypted card number. This can be decrypted using Relay decryption or using a function.

expiry.description: The expiry date of the card.

bin.description: The first 6 or 8 digits of the card number.

lastFour.description: The last 4 digits of the card number.

country.description: The country where the card was issued in lowercase ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

currency.description: The currency of the card in lowercase ISO 4217 format.

issuer.description: The name of the card issuer.
issuer.example: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

status.description:
The current status of the card:
  - `active` -- The card is active and can be used for transactions.
  - `replaced` -- The card has been replaced by another card (e.g. expired, lost or stolen).
  - `closed` -- The card account has been closed and can no longer be used for transactions.
  - `invalid` -- The card is invalid and cannot be used for transactions or updated.

replacement.description: The ID of the replacement card. This field is only present if the card has been replaced.

automaticUpdates.description: The status of Card Account Updater on this card. Evervault currently supports Card Account Updates for Visa, Mastercard and American Express cards.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp of when the card was created.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp of when the card was last updated.

example.issuer: Gringotts Wizarding Bank and Trust Company

updateType.description: The type of update to simulate.

number.description: The card number. Can be clear-text or an Evervault encrypted string.

updateType.description: The type of update to simulate.
The type of update to simulate.

type.description: The type of the selector used to identify the data on which the action should be performed.

role.description: The role of the encrypted field that must be matched for the operation to be performed.

selector.description:
The selector that identifies the data on which the action should be performed.
- If `type` is `json` then this should be a valid JSONPath string. This JSONPath selector will be used to match fields in the JSON payload in the request.
- When the `type` is set to `form`, the corresponding value should be a string. This string will be used to perform an exact match against the name of a form field.
- When the `type` is set to `header`, the corresponding value should be a string. This string will be used to perform case-insensitive match against the name of a header.

action.description: The operation to perform on the selected data.

selections.description: The selections of data on which the operation will be performed.

method.description: The HTTP method that must be matched for the operation to be performed. For any method, use null.

path.description: The path that must be matched for the operation to be performed. For wildcards, use '*'. For a catchall of all subsequent paths, use '**'.

request.description: The actions to be performed on the data on request.

response.description: The actions to be performed on the data on response.

id.description: The unique identifier for the Relay.

destinationDomain.description: The domain in front of which the Relay should be configured.

evervaultDomain.description: The Evervault managed domain to which requests to be relayed to the destination domain should be sent.

routes.description: A collection of route configurations for the Relay.

authentication.description: The type of authentication required for the Relay. If this is `null` then the Relay is unauthenticated.

encryptEmptyStrings.description: Whether or not empty strings should be encrypted.

app.description: The unique identifier for the app to which the Relay belongs.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Relay was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Relay was updated.

id.description: The unique identifier for the custom domain.

customDomain.description: The customer managed domain to which requests to be relayed to your domain should be sent.

relay.description: The ID of the Relay with which this custom domain is associated.

status.description: The status of the domains DNS verification.

validationRecord.description: Validation TXT record to be added on the `_ev-custom-relay` subdomain of your custom domain

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this custom domain was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this custom domain was last updated.

createdAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Webhook Endpoint was created.

updatedAt.description: The exact time, in epoch milliseconds, when this Webhook Endpoint was last updated.
